fields.description:
## Context
During review-threshold re-tuning (2026-07) we found that genuinely high-severity plan-review AND code-review findings systematically score priority 0.60-0.69 and fall just under the 0.70 block bar. Root cause: `priority = validity x impact`, and `impact` is the unweighted MEAN of 5 severity attributes (prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility). A finding that is critical on one axis but 'easy'/'local' on others gets averaged down (e.g. critical prod-impact + easy reversibility + module blast -> impact ~= 0.67), so real blocks land below the bar and ship as advisory.

We worked around it by lowering per-criterion block_thresholds to 0.60, but the aggregation itself is the deeper lever.

## Why revisit
- Threshold-lowering is a blunt fix; it also admits any finding that happens to average ~0.60.
- The mean conflates 'severe on the dimension that matters' with 'mild overall'. A max, weighted, or severity-gated aggregation (e.g. a critical prod_impact should floor impact regardless of reversibility) would let high-severity findings clear the bar on merit without lowering thresholds globally.
- Same mechanism affects code-review (findings cap ~0.67).

## Possible directions (to design later)
- Weighted aggregation (prod_impact / blast_radius weighted higher than reversibility).
- max-of-attributes or a severity-gated floor for critical/major.
- Decouple 'is it block-worthy' from 'validity x mean-impact' entirely (a learned/ruled block-worthiness signal).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Not scheduled — parking-lot idea. Revisit after the 0.60 threshold change has run in production and we can measure block-rate/precision under it.
